tasks.desc: A vulnerability exists in ExtractTileJPG() where JPEG tiles are not enforced to be read by the JPEG coder.
A stack read overflow occurs in MagickGetToken() when parsing a URL.
A vulnerability exists in the expr component where <, <=, >=, and > comparisons are not properly disallowed against empty value sets. While OVN expression syntax does not permit a literal empty value

The vulnerability occurs in tree mode parsing, where the code eagerly parses a tree entry's mode until it encounters a character outside the accepted set of octal digits '0' - '7'. If the provided buf
An off-by-one error exists in the output buffer check within the HighPassBand::decode() function of VC5Decompressor.
A vulnerability exists in the CFF implementation where the Charset handling allows for a buffer overrun.
A potential out of bounds error exists in packet-quakeworld.c, where an overflow can occur while writing to a buffer.

The RAR5 reader assumes that a block contains complete huffman table data. In cases where invalid files declare the existence of huffman tables but specify a block size too small to contain the full t
A vulnerability exists in the ReadTIFFImage() function where the alpha channel is incorrectly disassociated for images that do not have a photometric value of PHOTOMETRIC_RGB.

A vulnerability exists due to incorrect size calculations in the tables listing UTF-8 and Boolean properties, where the elements are guints rather than guint16's. This discrepancy can lead to issues o
The vulnerability allows the number of elements in the header_start array to exceed intended limits, potentially leading to security issues.
A buffer overflow exists in the dotnet module.
The avcodec/mlpdec component leaves an invalid num_primitive_matrices value in the context, which results in a runtime error: index 8 out of bounds for type 'uint8_t [8]'.
The vulnerability occurs when reading BAM files, as the qname is not properly NUL terminated. This can lead to possible invalid reads when printing names.
A vulnerability exists in NTF where a negative size can be passed to strncpy(), potentially leading to undefined behavior or security issues.

A vulnerability exists in idl/drsblobs where the number of schedules in a struct is incorrectly overwritten to 1, even when the struct actually contains zero or two schedules. This can lead to incorre
The function tui_copy_source_line in tui/tui-winsource.c contains a vulnerability where it can advance past the terminating \0 in its input string.
The dnie module does not properly check the length of uncompressed data, which can lead to a security vulnerability.
An off-by-one error exists in the imap function.

The vulnerability occurs in the bfd_check_format_matches function in format.c, where bfd_alloc memory is not properly released. When there is a match failure, memory can be released, but after a match

A buffer overflow vulnerability exists in readelf's process_mips_specific function due to the incorrect assumption that DT_MIPS_OPTIONS is a regular array. The function does not properly account for t
A heap-buffer-overflow occurs in the findNextBorderPixel() function due to a missing check of the pix boundary when searching for the next pixel.

A use-after-free vulnerability exists in readelf's process_archive functionality, where fuzzed thin archives trigger the error return path in setup_nested_archive. In elfcomm.c, the setup_nested_archi

The HTML parser exhibits a quadratic runtime vulnerability due to the removal of an important optimization that avoids repeatedly scanning the input buffer for terminating characters. This issue arise
A crash occurs in Assimp::ObjFileMtlImporter::getFloatValue, leading to a security vulnerability.
A vulnerability exists in the function fp_read_radix_16() where the write index is incorrectly updated during multiple writes into a word. If the last word is full and there is no more data to write,

A vulnerability exists in ms_adpcm where size checks use 'blockalign' (the size of a block) instead of 'samplesperblock', even though each block contains 7 samples per channel as part of the preamble.
Converting an empty rect to an irect does not preserve the coordinates.
A bounds check issue exists in the strptime function, which can lead to a security vulnerability.
An out of bounds read occurs in the tcos module.
A heap-buffer-overflow error occurs when decoding variable literal length.
A vulnerability exists in which there is no check for enough space to decode the cbytes token.
A vulnerability exists in the function ChannelMaskTlv::GetChannelMask() within meshcop-tlv, where the function does not correctly retrieve the channel mask.

An invalid cast occurs when a variable-length bytearray field is decoded as Decimal256 Arrow data in the Parquet C++ implementation. This can lead to incorrect processing of Parquet data containing De
The oberthur module does not correctly check for return values, which may lead to security vulnerabilities.
A heap-buffer-overflow occurs when reading the frame index chunk in the code, leading to a security vulnerability.

ArrayPrototype in LibJS is not an Array exotic object as required by the ECMAScript specification, which can lead to compatibility issues and unexpected behavior with JavaScript code that relies on Ar

A vulnerability exists where the delref operation occurs before successful allocation, which can lead to inconsistent reference counts after an out-of-memory (OOM) condition. This issue is particularl
In SamsungV2Decompressor::decompressRow(), during the "complex case" for the first 16 pixels of a row, motion (and thus slideOffset) may cause refpixel to point outside the row bounds.
A security vulnerability exists in the Shell where non-bareword nodes are allowed as part of a heredoc key.

A null pointer dereference occurs in the legacy SAX1 parser when parsing in SAX1 mode due to the use of namePush instead of nameNsPush. The unprefixed name from namePush pollutes the global namespace,
The vulnerability arises from not properly considering orientation in *OutBufferSize, which can lead to mismatches in computed strides and result in memory corruption and/or out-of-bounds writes.
An out of bounds access occurs when decoding the Private Extension IE in GTPv2.

A use after free vulnerability exists in compound division by zero, where destroying the result operand early can lead to accessing freed memory if the division fails. The division result is not safel
An incorrect population count occurs in the hash map implementation in map, where setting the same key twice causes the population to be incorrectly incremented.
The vulnerability in Cr2Decompressor::decodeN_X_Y() allows slices to not cover the whole image size.

A heap use-after-free vulnerability exists in the repacker due to storing a reference to the link in overflow records. The link object may be freed if the sorted graph vector is resized, leading to po
The code does not check whether a DefaultGray, DefaultRGB, or DefaultCMYK colour space is one of Lab, Pattern, or Indexed, nor does it verify that it has the correct number of components. This allows

A vulnerability exists in pattern step scaling where the YStep value in the pattern can be so large that, when scaled by the CTM, it overflows a float and becomes #INF. This causes all subsequent arit

A vulnerability exists in reading xref offsets where the code calculates available buffer space, but a malformed file can cause this calculation to result in negative values. These negative values are

A vulnerability exists in the eexec decoding process due to insufficient buffer bounds checking in the relevant code. The function fails to properly check for a zero length buffer in addition to a neg
The dxf2dwg tool does not honor the --force-free option when errors occur, potentially leading to improper memory management.
A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the function audio_sample_entry_read.
A buffer overflow exists in som_set_reloc_info in som.c, where the function accesses symbols past symcount and accesses fixup past end_fixups.

A vulnerability exists where the /Length value from the Encrypt dictionary is used for decryption, even for encryption types where this is not valid. The KeyLen value is incorrectly handled for encryp

An invalid memory read (heap-buffer-overflow) occurs in the ndpi_check_tinc function in src/lib/protocols/tinc.c at line 105. This vulnerability allows reading past the end of a heap-allocated buffer,

A vulnerability exists in LJpegDecompressor::decodeScan() where the LJpeg frame size is not properly validated. If the frame contains fewer pixels than the DNG tile or slice, this can result in uninit
A vulnerability exists in numeric.c within the cmpnum function, where mrb_bigint_cmp() is incorrectly called if v2 is bigint instead of v1.
A vulnerability exists in bigint.c within the mrb_bint_copy function, where the dup operation is not properly allowed for bigints.
A vulnerability exists where the buffer pointer is not set before parsing ASN1, which can lead to incorrect memory access during ASN1 parsing.

A vulnerability exists when handling corrupted TrueType fonts where a glyph claims to be a component glyph, but the component GID is larger than the number of glyphs in the font, making it invalid. Wh

A vulnerability exists when writing profiles if tables have different node count values in the code. Each table may have a different node count number, which can lead to errors during profile writing.

A vulnerability exists in the Type 1/2 charstring execution state stack due to an incorrect bounds checking macro, which is not used in the minimal CharString interpreter found in pdfwrite and related
A vulnerability exists where processing invalid UTF-8 sequences can result in reading past the start of the sequence.

A heap buffer overflow edge-case exists in the parse_msg() function, where a SIP message parsing error log can result in unsafe printing of a non-NULL terminated string. The OpenSIPS PKG memory alloca

A missing NULL terminator in the ieee1905_reporting_policy_flags[] array in packet-ieee1905.c causes a buffer overflow. This vulnerability is triggered during the execution of functions such as proto_
Uninitialized local variables in fuzz_die_cu_attrs.c and fuzz_die_cu_offset.c do not follow the requirements of libdwarf, potentially leading to security vulnerabilities.

A vulnerability exists in objdump.c where a memcpy operation in the slurp_symtab and slurp_dynamic_symtab functions can cause a segmentation fault if symcount or dynsymcount is -1, resulting in syms o
A heap-buffer-overflow vulnerability exists in the VXLAN de-tunneling logic of ndpiReader. Specifically, the function ndpi_is_valid_vxlan in ndpi/example/reader_util.c can read out of bounds, leading
A buffer overread occurs with HTML doctype declarations in cases of malloc failure.
A buffer overflow occurs in ecoff.c within the _bfd_ecoff_slurp_symbolic_info function due to string sections not being zero terminated.

A vulnerability exists in the BMP array header parsing code where the remaining data size is not checked before parsing in the relevant function. This can lead to out-of-bounds reads or crashes when h
A double-free vulnerability exists in indxf when handling INVALIDDWG cases. If dxfname is allocated and an import error occurs later, the memory may be freed twice.
In two particular cases in src/lib/libdwarf/dwarf_locationop_read.c, the necessary check for reading past the end is missing, which can lead to a security vulnerability.
A security vulnerability exists in kernel.c due to the absence of a `respond_to_missing?` method, which may cause objects to incorrectly report their ability to respond to certain methods.
An out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the utf8ToUtf16() function in poppler/UTF.cc. This issue is introduced by a recent regression and is detected by Valgrind as an invalid write of size 2,

A vulnerability exists in the xform fuzzer where the final transform operation does not match the intended behavior of being the same as the first transform operation but without TJXOPT_COPYNONE or TJ

A vulnerability exists in libsepol/cil where an anonymous classpermission can be passed into a macro that uses a classpermissionset rule. When this occurs, there is no way to distinguish between the a
A vulnerability exists in the inter-layer prediction functions of svcdec where NULL dereferencing can occur due to improper tracking of resolution level initialization.
A security vulnerability exists where the dateStyle and timeStyle parameters in the fuzzer are not limited to only valid enum values.
An index-out-of-bounds runtime error occurs in the function ixheaacd_res_ctns_apply when the value of the residual sampling frequency index exceeds the maximum residual sampling frequency index.
A bug in SkAnimatedImage::decodeNextFrame leads to uninitialized memory access, which can result in undefined behavior and potential security vulnerabilities.
The use of memcmp instead of strncmp in dict can result in out-of-bounds (OOB) reads when comparing strings.
An incomplete Mach-O universal object is not checked for very early in the process in src/lib/libdwarf/dwarf_machoread.c, which can lead to a security vulnerability.
An invalid free occurs in the function alpha_ecoff_get_relocated_section_contents in coff-alpha.c due to improper iteration through reloc_vector, potentially leading to memory corruption or crashes.
A heap-overflow by 1 occurs in the decode_r11 function.
A vulnerability exists where the format can be taken from foreign metadata while the subformat is taken from command-line options, resulting in a mismatch between format and subformat.
A security vulnerability exists in the bmp decoder due to an incorrect offset check.
A stack buffer overflow occurs in the pkcs15 module while parsing the DES initialization vector (iv).
A possible crash occurs in vvc tile parsing when handling corrupted streams.
A vulnerability exists in p_lx_elf.cpp where insufficient care is taken when recovering from slid b_info, potentially leading to incorrect handling of binary information.
A use-after-free (UAF) vulnerability exists in LibAudio when loading WAV metadata.
A buffer overflow exists in the `mrb_memsearch()` function.
A possible heap overflow exists in the mp3 reframer.
The vulnerability is a use of uninitialized memory in the function hScale16To15_c(), which occurs due to alloc_lines() not clearing allocated memory in swscale/slice.

A vulnerability exists where incorrect aber values are not properly cleaned, and insufficient extra data is allocated to the image, leading to a potential buffer overrun when processing specially craf

The vulnerability occurs in tools/target_dec_fuzzer where missing slices can have unpredictable content due to the use of uninitialized values. The buffer allocation does not match production code, wh

The tools/target_dec_fuzzer uses buffers that may contain unpredictable content due to missing slices, as it does not zero these buffers like the production code. This results in the use of uninitiali
